description_long: This object is a cluster of bluebonnet flowers, featuring tall, upright stems topped with dense, elongated racemes of vivid blue to violet pea-like blossoms. Each raceme is composed of numerous small, rounded petals arranged in a spiral pattern, with the uppermost petals often displaying a lighter, almost white tip. The foliage at the base consists of slender, palmate leaves with narrow, pointed leaflets radiating from a central point. The stems are green and sturdy, supporting the weight of the flower clusters above the foliage. The overall appearance is lush and vibrant, with the blue flowers contrasting sharply against the green leaves. This type of flower is commonly found in parks, gardens, and landscaped urban areas.
description: A vibrant cluster of bluebonnet flowers with tall stems, dense blue racemes, and slender palmate green leaves. The blue blossoms are tightly packed and stand out against the foliage.
description_view_0: Front view showing three main blue flower spikes with dense, rounded petals and palmate leaves radiating at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the staggered arrangement of the flower spikes and the spread of the palmate leaves.
description_view_2: Angled view emphasizing the height and density of the central flower spike, with leaves visible at the base.
description_view_3: Alternate front view with a clear display of the blue flower clusters and the symmetrical spread of the leaves.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a spiral raceme, with each small flower displaying bilateral symmetry and overlapping slightly along the central stem. The overall inflorescence is dense and elongated.
